title: Rhodococcum vitis-idaea
intro:
There is no such plant as Rhodococcum vitis-idaea. 

However, I found a plant called Rhodococcus which is a genus of bacteria, and Vitis-idaea which is a plant species of the genus Vaccinium, also known as the lingonberry.

If you meant the plant Vaccinium vitis-idaea, here is a paragraph about it:

Vaccinium vitis-idaea, also known as the lingonberry, is a low-growing, deciduous shrub native to cold, subarctic regions. The lingonberry has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, fever, and infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium vitis-idaea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made from lingonberry include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support immune function and overall health.
